page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong computational and memory performance. The Intel Xeon processor achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core (2211.0) and multi-core (20583.0) metrics, as well as a Passmark CPU score of 83187.08. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 5112.66 GB/sec and a large-block uncached read bandwidth of 197.6 GB/sec. The server also shows high efficiency in static web serving, reaching a throughput of 105.74 GB/sec, and handles Redis SET operations at 114.69 million operations per second.

The resource profile of the m8i-flex.16xlarge makes it highly suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, database operations, and memory-cached applications. The inclusion of Intel AMX instructions enables efficient CPU-based machine learning tasks, as evidenced by prompt processing speeds of 296.39 tokens per second on 7B LLM models. However, the absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs means workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O or heavy parallel graphics acceleration must rely on network-attached storage and external resources, utilizing the baseline 15 Gbps network bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose server built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, this instance includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, alongside strong memory read bandwidth of 197.6 GB/sec. With a baseline network speed of 15 Gbps and no local storage or GPUs, this instance is optimized for enterprise web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference. It delivers high throughput for static web serving at 105.74 GB/sec and handles Redis SET operations at 114.69 million operations per second, making it a robust choice for memory-cached applications and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers top-tier CPU and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor with Advanced Matrix Extensions.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring sixty-four virtual CPUs, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 480 MB L3 cache and AMX support
Top-tier Geekbench and Passmark CPU benchmark performance
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Suited for web serving, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference
